message:
test+deps(cass): cover empty-index-dir masquerade case; record rayon in Cargo.lock for frankensearch-lexical

Two small follow-ups to the preceding `fix(status/health/...):
stop creating an empty index dir when probing a fresh install` commit
(8bdf6c0a).

1. New test: `status_empty_index_dir_without_meta_still_reports_not_initialized`

   The prior commit's test covered the case where no `index/` tree exists
   at all. This new case covers the other half: a user who ran an older
   cass version (pre-fix) and has a stray `index/v7/` directory left on
   disk *without a meta.json* inside it. The fixture creates exactly that
   layout, runs `cass status --json`, and asserts:

     * `status == "not_initialized"`
     * `initialized == false`
     * `index.exists == false`
     * `recommended_action` contains `cass index --full` so the operator
       has a copy-paste path forward

   Without the preceding fix, the handler would have treated the empty
   versioned directory as a valid index root and muddled the "index
   exists but is unhealthy" vs. "not initialized" signal. Locking it
   down so a future refactor cannot regress.

2. Cargo.lock: record `rayon` as a dep of the frankensearch-lexical
   transitive crate

   Upstream `frankensearch` added a parallel writer path that brings
   `rayon` into the dependency graph via
   `frankensearch-lexical → rayon`. `cargo build` regenerated the lock
   file to reflect the new dep; this commit captures that so CI builds
   and `cargo install --locked` users see the same resolved versions.
   No change to `frankensearch`'s git revision — this is purely the
   lock-file side of a dep that the pinned revision already declared.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
